job_statement: When planning a GitOps deployment or upgrade, I want to verify component compatibility with my OpenShift version, so I can ensure all features will work correctly in my environment.
related_jobs: Evaluate whether to upgrade to a new GitOps version
desired_outcomes: Reduce time spent troubleshooting version mismatches
evidence: gitops-release-notes.adoc -> Section 'Compatibility and support matrix', line 21 [module: modules/go-compatibility-and-support-matrix.adoc, type: REFERENCE]
notes: Source module: modules/go-compatibility-and-support-matrix.adoc (REFERENCE). This is a critical planning job that affects deployment decisions.

job_statement: When evaluating new GitOps features, I want to identify which features are in Technology Preview versus General Availability, so I can decide whether they are safe to use in production.
desired_outcomes: Avoid using unsupported features in production
Plan for GA timeline of preview features
evidence: gitops-release-notes.adoc -> Section 'Technology Preview features', lines 42-79 [module: modules/go-compatibility-and-support-matrix.adoc, type: REFERENCE]
notes: Source module: modules/go-compatibility-and-support-matrix.adoc (REFERENCE). TP features include: custom must-gather tool, Argo Rollouts, ApplicationSet Progressive Rollout, multiple sources for application, and more.

job_statement: When a new GitOps version is released, I want to understand what new capabilities are available, so I can determine if they solve my current pain points or enable new use cases.
desired_outcomes: Identify features that solve current problems
evidence: gitops-release-notes.adoc -> Section 'New features 1.9.0', lines 28-50 [module: modules/gitops-release-notes-1-9-0.adoc, type: REFERENCE]
notes: Source module: modules/gitops-release-notes-1-9-0.adoc (REFERENCE). Key features: custom must-gather tool (TP), Argo Rollouts support (TP).

job_statement: When planning an upgrade, I want to know which features are being deprecated or removed, so I can prepare for migration and avoid breaking my existing deployments.
desired_outcomes: Ensure smooth transition to new configuration formats
evidence: gitops-release-notes.adoc -> Section 'Deprecated and removed features 1.9.0', lines 51-62 [module: modules/gitops-release-notes-1-9-0.adoc, type: REFERENCE]
notes: Source module: modules/gitops-release-notes-1-9-0.adoc (REFERENCE). Critical planning job: .spec.resourceCustomizations deprecated, sso/dex fields extending deprecation to 1.10.0.

job_statement: When experiencing issues with the current version, I want to check if my problem is a known bug that has been fixed in the new release, so I can decide if upgrading will resolve my issue.
related_jobs: Verify fix applies to my scenario
desired_outcomes: Confirm upgrade will solve my problem
evidence: gitops-release-notes.adoc -> Section 'Fixed issues 1.9.0', lines 64-78 [module: modules/gitops-release-notes-1-9-0.adoc, type: REFERENCE]
notes: Source module: modules/gitops-release-notes-1-9-0.adoc (REFERENCE). Fixed issues include: TLS certificate reload, GPG signature verification, TFS Git repository support, HA resource limits, namespace termination blocking.

job_statement: When planning to deploy or upgrade GitOps, I want to be aware of known issues and their workarounds, so I can avoid or mitigate problems proactively.
desired_outcomes: Avoid hitting known issues in production
Reduce incident response time with pre-planned workarounds
evidence: gitops-release-notes.adoc -> Section 'Known issues 1.10', lines 79-129 [module: modules/gitops-release-notes-1-9-0.adoc, type: REFERENCE]
notes: Source module: modules/gitops-release-notes-1-9-0.adoc (REFERENCE). Known issue: Argo CD not reading TLS certificates from argocd-tls-certs-cm config map. Workaround provided with SSL_CERT_DIR env var.

job_statement: When managing progressive application rollouts, I want to understand how to use ApplicationSet Progressive Rollout Strategy, so I can deploy updates incrementally instead of simultaneously.
desired_outcomes: Reduce blast radius of failed deployments
evidence: gitops-release-notes.adoc -> Section 'New features 1.8.0', lines 11-22 [module: modules/gitops-release-notes-1-8-0.adoc, type: REFERENCE]
notes: Source module: modules/gitops-release-notes-1-8-0.adoc (REFERENCE). Technology Preview feature in 1.8.0. Enables progressive rollouts instead of simultaneous updates.

job_statement: When running GitOps on ARM architecture, I want to understand platform compatibility and limitations, so I can deploy successfully on ARM-based OpenShift clusters.
evidence: gitops-release-notes.adoc -> Section 'New features 1.8.0', lines 26-31 [module: modules/gitops-release-notes-1-8-0.adoc, type: REFERENCE]
notes: Source module: modules/gitops-release-notes-1-8-0.adoc (REFERENCE). Important: spec.sso.provider: keycloak not yet supported on ARM.

job_statement: When monitoring Argo CD components, I want to enable workload monitoring and alerts for my instances, so I can detect when component replicas drift from desired state.
evidence: gitops-release-notes.adoc -> Section 'New features 1.8.0', lines 33-33 [module: modules/gitops-release-notes-1-8-0.adoc, type: REFERENCE]
notes: Source module: modules/gitops-release-notes-1-8-0.adoc (REFERENCE). Set .spec.monitoring.enabled to true to create PrometheusRule with alert rules for each component.

job_statement: When managing large Git repositories with manifests, I want to configure repo server arguments for maximum manifest size, so I can prevent failures when processing large directory structures.
parent_job: Configure Argo CD for enterprise-scale repositories
evidence: gitops-release-notes.adoc -> Section 'New features 1.8.0', lines 35-50 [module: modules/gitops-release-notes-1-8-0.adoc, type: REFERENCE]
notes: Source module: modules/gitops-release-notes-1-8-0.adoc (REFERENCE). Example shows --max.combined.directory.manifests.size configuration via extraRepoCommandArgs.

job_statement: When upgrading GitOps versions, I want to understand how the skipRange annotation affects my upgrade path, so I can plan for direct upgrades skipping intermediate versions.
parent_job: Plan and execute GitOps operator upgrade
evidence: gitops-release-notes.adoc -> Section 'New features 1.5.0', lines 28-28 [module: modules/gitops-release-notes-1-5-0.adoc, type: REFERENCE]
notes: Source module: modules/gitops-release-notes-1-5-0.adoc (REFERENCE). CSV has olm.skipRange: '>=1.0.0 <1.5.0' allowing direct upgrade to 1.5.0.

job_statement: When integrating Argo CD with OpenShift authentication, I want to use RH-SSO to log in with OpenShift credentials including kube:admin, so I can provide seamless access using existing identity infrastructure.
parent_job: Configure enterprise authentication for Argo CD
related_jobs: Set up proxy configuration for SSO
evidence: gitops-release-notes.adoc -> Section 'New features 1.5.0', lines 30-36 [module: modules/gitops-release-notes-1-5-0.adoc, type: REFERENCE]
notes: Source module: modules/gitops-release-notes-1-5-0.adoc (REFERENCE). RH-SSO v7.5.1 update enables OpenShift login, RBAC with groups, and HTTP_Proxy support.

job_statement: When addressing security vulnerabilities, I want to know what security updates are included in the release, so I can prioritize upgrades based on CVE severity.
parent_job: Maintain security compliance for GitOps infrastructure
evidence: gitops-release-notes.adoc -> Section 'RHSA-2023:5029 security update advisory', lines 15-29 [module: modules/gitops-release-notes-1-9-2.adoc, type: REFERENCE]
notes: Source module: modules/gitops-release-notes-1-9-2.adoc (REFERENCE). Includes RHSA link and command to view container images.

job_statement: When implementing continuous deployment for cloud native applications, I want to understand what GitOps can help me achieve, so I can determine if it fits my use case.
desired_outcomes: Ensure consistent application state across environments
notes: Overview section describes core GitOps capabilities: consistency across clusters, recovery/recreation, configuration changes, templated config, and promotion across environments.
